description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with a single build stage that defines six jobs: ubuntu-22.04-s390x-all-linux, ubuntu-22.04-s390x-all-system, ubuntu-22.04-s390x-alldbg, ubuntu-22.04-s390x-clang, ubuntu-22.04-s390x-notcg, and ubuntu-22.04-s390x-tci, all extending .custom_runner_template and running on runners tagged ubuntu_22.04 and s390x. Each job has no needs and is triggered only when the project namespace is "qemu-project" and the branch matches ^staging or when the $S390X_RUNNER_AVAILABLE variable is true; the alldbg, clang, notcg, and tci jobs are manual and allow failure. In every job the script starts with "mkdir build" and "cd build", then runs "../configure" with job‑specific options (e.g., --enable-debug --disable-system --disable-tools --disable-docs for all-linux, --disable-user for all-system, --enable-debug for alldbg, --cc=clang --cxx=clang++ --enable-ubsan for clang, --disable-tcg for notcg, --enable-tcg-interpreter for tci) and aborts printing config.log and meson logs on failure. After configuration each job runs "make --output-sync -j`nproc`"; the all-linux job also runs "make --output-sync check-tcg" and "make --output-sync -j`nproc` check", the all-system and clang jobs run "make --output-sync -j`nproc` check", the alldbg job runs "make clean" before the build and then "make --output-sync -j`nproc` check", and the tci job only builds without any check step. No artifacts, caches, or deployment targets are defined.
